devices: Polanyi/Fraser structural-commons naming ADD (the memo's highest-value, lowest-cost recommendation and the lens's biggest ratified gap): a new paragraph in §The Second Cycle names Fraser's expropriation-vs-exploitation distinction (value extracted *through* the wage contract vs *around* it) and Polanyi's fictitious-commodity insight, then states the META-CLAIM directly — 'The reef and the atmosphere and the community and the worker's body and the inheritance of the next generation are, for the purposes of this accounting, the same kind of object,' and 'What the diagnostic tradition from Polanyi to Fraser named, this book proposes to price and to set aside in advance.' This is measurement-not-diagnosis stated against its own lineage.

Coase absent-parties forward-link (memo ADD (b)): the 'voluntary... and, in its own terms, honest' sentence now names the two parties not at the table (the miner's future body; the unborn inheriting generation) and forward-links 'why a market cannot price what the absent parties would have refused' — seeds the future-can't-bid Coase contradiction without fully closing it here (deferred to Ch5).

Configurational/extension framing held light: Mazzucato EXTEND (financial-capital rent -> physical-resource extraction) + Harvey PARALLEL kept as a single compressed lineage note; the integrated-config apparatus (CIT, Four Gates, etc.) correctly NOT imported into the human-case chapter.
Spatial cost severance + intergenerational cost severance retained as the chapter's named concealment-arrangement contribution; Ch7 forward-link kept.
Purdue second-cycle as the culture-not-structure refutation (engaged forcefully); mechanism-not-motive and agency-with-limits objections engaged.
voice_notes: Provisional voice applied: zero em-dashes and zero en-dashes (verified 0/0 — required converting two citation-gap range glyphs to 'to' and dropping the original em-dash 'End of Chapter' marker). Globally Inclusive Plain English with memoir/literary latitude preserved: kept the scene-and-rhythm beats (Kennedy drive, Bailey 'almost like drowning', Latusek 'bag over my face', the orange creeks, 'the value dispersed, the costs stayed in the hollows'), natural contractions, no regionalisms or idiom. Terms are built-then-named (cost severance defined from the worked case before the label). The new Polanyi/Fraser paragraph was written in the same plain register — defines expropriation in-line ('accumulation by means other than the wage bargain') rather than assuming the reader knows Fraser. No clipped essay-economy; sentences allowed to breathe at the landing points.
where_lens_held: The lens drove almost every editorial decision without ambiguity. The four-beat spine and cut list mapped cleanly onto concrete prose moves; the canonical-figure ledger resolved every figure question (what to keep, what must stay Ch8/TA-only, the 'late 1980s' phasing); the owner-map gave an unambiguous RELOCATE target for the rental-housing thesis; and the META-CLAIMS told me exactly what the Polanyi/Fraser ADD had to *say* (same-object structural commons + price-what-they-only-named), not just that it belonged here. The objection list told me which objections this chapter owns (mechanism-not-motive, culture-not-structure, paternalism, Coase-seed) versus which to forward-link. For a term-coining human-case chapter this was enough to draft confidently.
where_lens_strained: Three places the memo + lens were NOT fully sufficient and would benefit from an author chat. (1) The Polanyi/Fraser ADD: the lens names them as 'the biggest gap' and says price-what-they-named, and the memo green-lights a one-sentence Fraser seed — but I wrote a full ~150-word paragraph asserting a specific Fraser reading (expropriation = accumulation 'by means other than the wage bargain') and a specific Polanyi reading (fictitious commodity), with NO citation to a specific work or page, and the NO-FABRICATION rule bars me from inventing one. Whether this much weight on Polanyi/Fraser belongs in the human-case chapter (vs. a lighter seed handed to Ch1/Ch5 where the matrix work concentrates) is a genuine editorial-judgment + book-level-positioning call the author should make; I leaned toward the fuller naming because the lens flagged it as the single biggest ratified gap and this is the named right-home, but I flag the size as the author's to ratify. The Fraser/Polanyi claims also need real citations [the paragraph currently rests on uncited characterizations of both thinkers]. (2) The financialization/Pistor objection: the lens says 'answer via Polanyi re-embedding' and the owner-map says the re-embedding answer lives in Ch9 — so I did NOT stage it in Ch2, but the lens's instruction to name Polanyi *here* sits in slight tension with deferring the re-embedding *answer* to Ch9; whether Ch2's Polanyi naming should gesture forward to that answer is a coordination call across chapters. (3) The combustion-tail / carbon figure boundary: canon is unambiguous that $496/$510/RCV are Ch8/TA-only and Ch2 must only say 'we will return to that,' which I obeyed — but the 33-to-122x band IS in Ch2 and depends on 'which estimate you use for the value of the harm when the coal is burned,' which is the SCC; whether even that oblique gesture risks pulling the carbon number forward is a fine-grained call I resolved conservatively but the author may want to inspect. Net: lens-driven drafting substantially reduced the need for a per-chapter author chat (the structural, figure, cut, voice, and objection decisions were all resolvable from the materials), but it did NOT eliminate it — the Polanyi/Fraser ADD's scale + missing citations is exactly the kind of judgment that still needs the author.

voice_notes: Globally Inclusive Plain English + literary/memoir latitude applied: zero em-dashes and zero en-dashes in body prose (the single remaining em-dash is the house structural end-marker '— End of Chapter 4 —', matching the book convention and not prose). Em-dashes converted to commas/colons/periods/restructured sentences. Natural contractions retained. Kept scene and rhythm (the Ekofisk Christmas opening, 'man, woman, child, pensioner, newborn', 'the fund does not pull that carbon back out of the sky') rather than clipping to essay economy. Jargon built-then-named (Hartwick rule, accountability bond B, scarcity rent all glossed before use). No regional idiom. No new named subjects; Saro-Wiwa retained as deceased public-figure on the historical record per the named-subject discipline.
where_lens_held: The lens carried the chapter cleanly on its core jobs. The three meta-claims mapped directly onto the existing spine: measurement-not-diagnosis + conservative-by-construction is exactly what the differentiation proof (small CS for Norway, enormous for Nigeria) demonstrates, and the falsifiability badge made that explicit. Configurational-novelty was the biggest free win the memo predicted: the chapter was already doing Hartwick/Hotelling/Daly/Parfit work uncredited, and naming them cost ~15-40 words each while massively strengthening lit-positioning, with no fabrication (the substance was already in the prose). The canonical-figure ledger and owner-map resolved every redundancy question without an author chat: Social Security depth -> Ch5, financialization/Polanyi/Fraser/Pistor -> Ch9, public-choice -> Ch5+appendix. The no-maximum discipline was decisive: ignoring the memo's '~3,200w landing' target and instead cutting only item-by-item let the chapter grow where naming and badging earned room.
where_lens_strained: Three places needed author judgment the memo+lens could not fully settle. (1) F-1, the definitional dependency: the lens told me B and residual commons value are load-bearing, but only the author/Ch5-Ch6 can decide whether to (a) push a first definition earlier into Ch3, (b) accept my in-prose recall gloss as sufficient for a reader-in-order, or (c) restructure. I wrote the gloss as the lowest-risk option and flagged it, but this is a genuine cross-chapter architecture call. (2) The financialization seam (lens meta-claim #1, the structural-commons/fictitious-commodity 'biggest gap'): the lens names Polanyi/Fraser as ratified fill and says name them 'where the chapter is the right home', but the owner-map routes the financialization/re-embedding ANSWER to Ch9. I resolved the tension by giving Ch4 a compact in-place answer (routing-device-not-severing-device) and flagging the full Polanyi/Fraser/Pistor naming to Ch9, but whether Ch4 should actually NAME Polanyi/Fraser here (vs only in Ch9) is a real editorial fork the author should rule on, especially since the lens calls this the biggest gap. (3) Load-bearing figures the lens did not pin: the B-is-large number (prior '70-80 cents on the dollar' vs my softened 'large majority') and the fund-size/per-citizen figures all need real sources before submission; I marked them as citation gaps rather than guess, which is correct under no-fabrication but leaves a verification task no lens can discharge. Net read: lens-driven drafting eliminated the need for a structural/spine author chat (the three-beat proof, the cuts, the relocations, and the named-positioning are all decidable from memo+lens), but a SHORT targeted chat is still warranted on exactly two points: the F-1 definitional placement and the Polanyi/Fraser name-here-vs-Ch9 fork.

voice_notes: Provisional voice applied throughout. ZERO em-dashes and ZERO en-dashes in the prose body (verified by grep: every one of the original chapter's heavy dash usages converted to commas, colons, periods, or restructured sentences; numeric ranges rendered 'forty to eighty,' 'six-to-seven,' 'four to six,' never with an en-dash). The only dashes remaining in the file are inside editorial [CITATION GAP]/[RELOCATE] bracket-flags and the original '— End of Chapter 5 —' sign-off marker, both of which are scaffolding, not prose. Idiom-free, non-regional, define-don't-assume; genuine terms built-then-named (cost severance, fictitious commodity, the two bonds). Natural contractions used. Literary/memoir latitude exercised at the human beats: the Libby 'still there, still dying, still counting its dead' cadence preserved; the Butler twenty-thousand-hours passage kept its rhythm; the Polanyi naming given room to breathe. Length is an OUTPUT (8,822w) not a target: I cut only the items the memo named as non-earning and then GREW the synthesis where the Polanyi/Fraser naming and the falsifiability + measurement-not-diagnosis beats needed room. I did not aim at the memo's ~6,000-6,500w recommended landing; the result sits above it because the spine material plus the ratified Polanyi/Fraser fill earned the space.
where_lens_held: The lens was strongly sufficient for the structural surgery. The deep-pass memo's issue list + cut list told me exactly which scenes/digressions did not earn their place (the two objection-apparatus blocks, the four etymology essays, the cadence tic, the seam bridges, the Sweden inventory), and the meta-claims + matrix-cell map told me what to foreground and where to insert the Polanyi/Fraser anchor (the synthesis paragraph, exactly as the memo's §10 HIGH flag predicted). The owner-map resolved every redundancy question without an author chat: Sweden->Ch9, McDowell carbon->Ch8, Public-Choice stays in Ch5, mask-veil->Ch1, thought-experiment->Ch7. The canonical-figure ledger let me confirm Ch5 carries no McDowell numerics to drift on. The objections list told me to name Coase, add the Pigou pointer, add the Sandel beat, and route financialization through Polanyi re-embedding. For all of this, memo + lens together were enough to draft confidently with no open structural question.
where_lens_strained: Three places where memo+lens were NOT enough and a per-chapter author chat would still be needed. (1) The Social Security $108T figure: the lens lists it as a canonical-adjacent number but the memo correctly flags it as a counterfactual-model output that is the chapter's single highest numbers-are-invented risk. I could flag it but I cannot resolve it: the author must decide whether to disclose the equity-return-series assumptions inline or demote it to a footnoted band. No lens input substitutes for that editorial call. (2) The Polanyi/Fraser fill is ratified as a gap-to-close, but the lens gives the meta-claim, not the author's preferred DEPTH or phrasing. I wrote a one-paragraph naming that I believe is load-bearing and correctly placed, but whether the author wants Polanyi/Fraser as a single synthesis beat (my choice) or a threaded motif across the chapter, and how much weight 'expropriation vs exploitation' should carry, is a voice/positioning judgment that needs the author. (3) The Sandel commodification tension at the Butler peak: I judged it worth naming inline with a short answer, because it sits exactly at the emotional peak and a Sandel reader will feel it. But the memo flags it as 'exposed and latent-risky' and notes the full engagement is owned downstream. Whether naming it here strengthens the chapter or invites an objection the chapter then cannot fully answer is a genuine editorial fork I resolved one way; the author may want it removed or expanded. Net: lens-driven drafting reduced the need for an author chat substantially (the whole structural cut/restructure ran without one), but it did NOT eliminate it. The residual chat is small and specific: three editorial calls (the $108T disclosure form, the Polanyi depth, the Sandel-beat placement) plus ratification of the citation-gap remediation plan. That is the honest test signal: the lens handles structure and positioning; it does not handle the figure-risk disclosure decision or the finest-grain voice/depth calls.

voice_notes: Body prose is clean of em-dashes and en-dashes (converted to commas/colons/periods/restructure throughout; the through-question 'Am I looking at the full honest accounting?' is voiced in the opener). Idiom-free, jargon built-then-named (foreclosure cost, externality tail, residual commons value, Commons Inversion Test all defined before/as named). Natural contractions kept. Literary latitude exercised lightly and appropriately for a methods chapter: the 'flashlight in a warehouse' image, the 'same object' enumeration cadence (reef/atmosphere/hillside/labor/generation), the weather-model and Yeager analogies retained. REMAINING em/en dashes (31 total) live ONLY in: (a) section headers '### Approach 1 — ...' and the bold Method-name labels '**Method 1 — Replacement Cost.**' — these match the existing chapter's own structural-label convention and are not running prose; (b) the convergence table cells — source-faithful canonical data formatting; (c) inside my own '[CITATION GAP: ...]' / '[RELOCATE TO ...]' editorial annotation brackets, which are not prose. If the morning voice confirmation wants ZERO dashes even in structural labels, the header/Method-label conversion is a trivial mechanical pass.
where_lens_held: The lens held strongly across the structural spine and the three meta-claims. Meta-claim 1 (structural-commons/fictitious-commodity) gave me an unambiguous home for the Polanyi/Fraser naming — the memo identified the extraction-commons section as the single best insertion point and the lens told me exactly what work it does (diagnosis names; framework prices+reserves), so I could draft it without an author chat. Meta-claim 2 (configurational novelty) mapped cleanly onto the existing §Contribution 'three additions' + Hotelling Identity material — the lens confirmed which positioning to keep (extending Pigou+Ostrom into extraction settings) and which naming-defense apparatus to evict to the TA. Meta-claim 3 (measurement-not-diagnosis, conservative-by-construction) is exactly the Harvey 'measures-the-mechanism-it-diagnoses' framing, which slotted in beside Polanyi/Fraser. The canonical figure ledger was decisive: it resolved the memo's open question ('does Ch6 carry $496/$510 explicitly?') — the owner-map line 'Ch6 uses the canonical figures' settled it, so I dropped the dual-basis national-vs-Pocahontas digression entirely and stated $496/$510/$504-518 directly. The objection list told me which flanks to close (Pistor, Coase) and which to leave deferred (ESG, degrowth, too-statist), matching the memo's EXPOSED-vs-ENGAGED audit.
where_lens_strained: Three places the memo+lens were NOT fully sufficient and would need an author chat. (1) FIGURE RECONCILIATION DOWNSTREAM: the lens gave me the canonical McDowell $496/$510, but the memo's §10 flags that Ch8's main-repo copy may still carry a pre-fix $510-carbon/$524-total version. I applied the canonical set to Ch6 confidently, but whether Ch8 has actually been cascaded to match is something I cannot verify from the lens alone — if Ch8 is stale, my Ch6 forward-reference ('Chapter 8 walks the full decomposition that produces these canonical figures') points at a number that does not yet exist there. This needs author/cross-session confirmation, not lens-driven drafting. (2) THE M1 PROSE RANGE: the canonical ledger gives RCV-band $580-620 as TA-only and the table M1 cell as $261-$2,412, but for the Method 1 PROSE I had to characterize the per-ton-of-coal range verbally ('low hundreds to roughly two thousand'); the lens did not give me a sanctioned prose phrasing for the M1 band, so I chose a conservative paraphrase that is consistent with the table but is a judgment call an author might want to set precisely. (3) THE POLANYI/FRASER DEPTH-VS-OWNERSHIP BOUNDARY: the lens says 'NAME them where the chapter is the right home' and flags Ch9 for the financialization/re-embedding answer — but the boundary between 'name the structural claim here' (Ch6) and 'develop the re-embedding answer there' (Ch9) is a judgment about how much Polanyi/Fraser Ch6 should carry vs Ch9. I drafted Ch6 to carry the naming + a one-paragraph Pistor engagement and defer the full double-movement development to Ch9; whether that division is where the author wants the seam is the kind of cross-chapter allocation call the memo cannot fully settle. Net signal: lens-driven drafting got Ch6 to a ratify-ready state on the spine, the cuts, the figures, and the gap-fills WITHOUT needing a per-chapter chat — the residual need-for-chat is concentrated entirely at cross-chapter seams (Ch8 figure cascade status, Ch9 re-embedding allocation), not at anything internal to Ch6.

voice_notes: Globally Inclusive Plain English with literary/memoir latitude applied: zero em-dashes and en-dashes throughout (converted the original's heavy em-dash usage to commas/periods/restructure; even the closing 'End of Chapter 8' rule de-dashed); natural contractions; jargon built-then-named (Pigouvian, Hotelling, fictitious-commodity each glossed in plain terms on first use); idiom-free and non-regional. Kept scene and rhythm (the orange creek, the West Fork in February, the rail car clearing the last tunnel) rather than essay-tight economy. The Dunbar couplet preserved for its emotional beat.
where_lens_held: The lens held strongly on positioning and structure: the deep-pass memo + lens + ledger together fully determined the four cuts/relocations, every figure value, the keystone-number reconciliation, the Polanyi/Fraser fill language (the lens supplied near-verbatim the same-object claim and the double-movement re-embedding framing), and which matrix cells Ch8 should newly name (Pigou-extend, Hotelling, falsifiability) versus correctly defer (Coase/Parfit/Ostrom/Hartwick to Ch6/Ch9). The redundancy-owner map cleanly resolved the housing (->Ch9), 2008 (->Ch9), Public-Choice (->Ch9), and mask-lineage (->Ch1) relocations without judgment calls. I never needed to guess on a figure or a positioning move.
where_lens_strained: Three places where the memo+lens were NOT enough and an author chat would still be needed: (1) The Polanyi/Fraser fill is RATIFIED in principle and the lens gives the conceptual content, but its canonical HOME is listed as Ch1/Ch5/Ch6/TA — NOT Ch8. I judged Ch8 the right concrete demonstration home (the 'second transaction' sentence IS the thesis in plain English) and wrote a full new section, but whether the book wants the first full Polanyi/Fraser exposition to land in Ch8 vs be established earlier and merely referenced here is an architecture decision only the author can make; if it lands in Ch1 first, my section should shrink to a callback. (2) The recommended landing in the memo was ~4,100w (Tier-1 SAFE + relocate 2008 + Political-Capture, HOLD the Dunbar trim). The no-maximum rule overrides the number, but the memo's own recommendation implies a TIGHTER chapter than I produced; the tension between 'add the ratified Polanyi/Fraser beat' (grows it) and 'recommended ~4,100w' (shrinks it) is a real editorial-altitude choice the author should confirm — I resolved it toward substance-earns-length, but reasonable editors differ. (3) The relocations I flagged (YIMBY, 2008, Public-Choice) are MOVES, not executions — per instructions I left them as inline flags rather than cutting blind, but the author/Ch9 session must actually absorb them or Ch8 will read as having dropped promised material; the flags assume a coordinated Ch9 pass that has not happened yet.

ESG/reformist-capture objection newly engaged at the end of Entering Wedges via the same re-embedding test
Formal-machinery callback added (Hotelling-identity bridge + ARR from Ch8/Appendix stand behind the four steps) so the practical chapter ties to its analytical spine
Closing reef/atmosphere/community/labor/future-generation one-object line echoes the Polanyi fictitious-commodity claim, binding the new fill to the existing hinge
voice_notes: Globally Inclusive Plain English with literary/memoir latitude applied. ZERO em-dashes and en-dashes in body, headers, and end-line (verified by grep; sibling Ch8 test-draft confirmed the corpus uses colon-style headers 'Step 1: Classify' and a plain 'End of Chapter' line, which I matched). Em-dash work converted to commas, colons, periods, and restructured clauses. No new idiom/colloquialism/regionalism introduced. Natural contractions kept. Room for rhythm and image preserved (the reserve-as-free-option beat, the 'one object, severed in one way, measured by one ledger' close) without the essays' clipped economy. No invented facts, scenes, quotes, dates, or biographical specifics; every figure traces to the existing chapter or the canonical ledger. No named people added; existing named-subject handling (none living/private newly named) preserved.
where_lens_held: The lens carried the redraft cleanly on the three hardest jobs. (1) Figure-correction was mechanical once the canonical ledger was in hand: the $550/$580-600 drift and the $40/$4.50 era-muddle both resolved to $510 + $4.50 with no judgment call needed. (2) The cut/relocate decisions were almost entirely pre-adjudicated by the deep-pass memo's SAFE/TRADE tagging and owner-map, so I rarely had to guess what earns its place. (3) Most importantly, the Polanyi/Fraser re-embedding fill -- the book's ratified biggest gap -- had an obvious structural home: the four steps ARE the re-embedding move (classify re-embeds in horizon, reserve in future claims, the bond in community), so the meta-claim wrote the section's spine, and the financialization/Pistor + commodify-labor objections both fell out of the SAME re-embedding polarity rather than needing separate ad hoc answers. The through-question (forward+backward) slotted naturally into the closing and the Darity/Restitution material. The lens genuinely reduced the need for an author chat on structure, figures, and positioning.
where_lens_strained: Three places where the memo+lens were NOT enough and an author chat is the right next step. (1) RELOCATION vs DELETION ambiguity for the healthcare/eleven-cent section: the owner-map says 'relocate OUT of Ch9 to Ch4,' but the source also presents it as load-bearing gut-punch material ('you already pay Swedish taxes'). I removed it wholesale and folded the residue into a Ch4 reference, but whether Ch4 actually wants the FULL section (vs. it becoming a standalone essay seed) is an author/cross-chapter call I cannot make from here, and I could not verify Ch4 does not already carry it. (2) The CCZ/Clarion-Clipperton material: the memo calls it a SAFE cut (re-run of the spine), but it is ALSO the only place the chapter exercises the framework on a live, contested, near-term decision and cross-references Ch7's Europa stress test -- an author may judge that worth keeping as the one 'framework applied in real time' beat. I cut it per the memo; this is a genuine TRADE the author should ratify or reverse. (3) VOICE confirmation is explicitly provisional: the literary/memoir-latitude register vs the essays' tight economy is the author's morning call, and the zero-dash rule forced several comma-heavy appositive strings (e.g. 'Groundwater in fossil aquifers, the Ogallala, the North China Plain, the Guarani, regenerates...') that read slightly list-like without the dashes the source leaned on -- an author may prefer a light restructure of those specific sentences. Also: the new re-embedding section is the largest piece of genuinely new prose (~900w); while every CLAIM in it is sourced to Polanyi/Fraser/Pistor/Darity already in the lens, its EMPHASIS and how hard to lean on the double-movement framing is a positioning choice the author should weigh, especially against whichever chapter currently owns Fraser/expropriation (the memo flags the fill should also land there).
